be excluded from the animal species. Take the birds of the Earth
and try to find out their number. We are of course attempting fi9
perform impossible things. Nobody has ever attempted to count or
can safely count the numbers of animals, much less of birds and insects.
. Granting their number, for the sake of simple calculation, to be more
than 1,000,000 times the number of animals, and supposing for a
single moment that the Earth's mass is composed of bird matter alone,
